verses.text: He sends for a colt, bidding them say, 'the Lord hath need of him.' Royal need. The King claims what He requires for His entry.
He sends for a colt, bidding them say, 'the Lord hath need of him.' Royal need. The King claims what He requires for His entry.
He sends for a colt, bidding them say, 'the Lord hath need of him.' Royal need. The King claims what He requires for His entry.
They find the colt and loose it, just as He said. Exact word. His foreknowledge is confirmed in every detail.
They find the colt and loose it, just as He said. Exact word. His foreknowledge is confirmed in every detail.
They find the colt and loose it, just as He said. Exact word. His foreknowledge is confirmed in every detail.
They throw garments on the colt, crying, 'Hosanna; Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.' Glad welcome. The crowds hail their lowly King.
They throw garments on the colt, crying, 'Hosanna; Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.' Glad welcome. The crowds hail their lowly King.
They throw garments on the colt, crying, 'Hosanna; Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.' Glad welcome. The crowds hail their lowly King.
They throw garments on the colt, crying, 'Hosanna; Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.' Glad welcome. The crowds hail their lowly King.
He enters the temple, looks round on all, and returns to Bethany. Searching eye. The Lord surveys His house before He acts.
Finding a leafy fig tree with no fruit, He curses it. A living parable. Profession without fruit invites the curse of God.
Finding a leafy fig tree with no fruit, He curses it. A living parable. Profession without fruit invites the curse of God.
Finding a leafy fig tree with no fruit, He curses it. A living parable. Profession without fruit invites the curse of God.
He casts out the traders, 'My house... the house of prayer; but ye have made it a den of thieves.' Holy zeal. Christ will not have worship turned into traffic.
He casts out the traders, 'My house... the house of prayer; but ye have made it a den of thieves.' Holy zeal. Christ will not have worship turned into traffic.
He casts out the traders, 'My house... the house of prayer; but ye have made it a den of thieves.' Holy zeal. Christ will not have worship turned into traffic.
The scribes seek to destroy Him, yet fear Him, for the people were astonished at His teaching. Helpless malice. They cannot touch Him while the crowd hangs on His word.
The scribes seek to destroy Him, yet fear Him, for the people were astonished at His teaching. Helpless malice. They cannot touch Him while the crowd hangs on His word.
In the morning the fig tree is dried up from the roots; Peter marvels. Swift judgment. The barren tree withers wholly, root and branch.
In the morning the fig tree is dried up from the roots; Peter marvels. Swift judgment. The barren tree withers wholly, root and branch.
'Have faith in God'; the believing prayer that doubts not removes mountains. Power of faith. What we ask, believing, God is able to give.
'Have faith in God'; the believing prayer that doubts not removes mountains. Power of faith. What we ask, believing, God is able to give.
'Have faith in God'; the believing prayer that doubts not removes mountains. Power of faith. What we ask, believing, God is able to give.
When ye pray, forgive, that your Father may forgive you. Forgiving prayer. An unforgiving heart cannot hold God's pardon.
When ye pray, forgive, that your Father may forgive you. Forgiving prayer. An unforgiving heart cannot hold God's pardon.
Asked by what authority, He asks of John's baptism, from heaven or of men? A wise counter. He exposes their insincerity by their own question.
Asked by what authority, He asks of John's baptism, from heaven or of men? A wise counter. He exposes their insincerity by their own question.
Asked by what authority, He asks of John's baptism, from heaven or of men? A wise counter. He exposes their insincerity by their own question.
Asked by what authority, He asks of John's baptism, from heaven or of men? A wise counter. He exposes their insincerity by their own question.
They reason and dare not answer; so neither will He tell them. Cornered. Those who hide from the truth forfeit further light.
They reason and dare not answer; so neither will He tell them. Cornered. Those who hide from the truth forfeit further light.
